business.headline: Govt caps duty-free gold imports at 100 kg under Advance Authorisation schemeGold imports in India, the world's second-biggest gold consumer after China, are driven by the jewellery industry demand. Such imports involve a substantial outflow of foreign exchange.

US moving to end $265 million fraud cases against Gautam Adani: ReportThe US Justice Department is reportedly set to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Gautam Adani linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report.

We asked top banks how they fight digital fraud. None respondedIn the third and final part of the series, Banking Risks in Digital India, IndiaToday.in reached out to major banks to understand how they are preparing for the next generation of digital fraud — from deepfakes and mule accounts to coercion-driven scams and AI-enabled deception. None of the banks responded.

Air India reports $2.8 billion loss in FY26, biggest since Tata takeoverAccording to Singapore Airlines' annual report, Air India's losses stood at 3.56 billion Singapore dollars during the financial year.

Paytm's pivotal year: How Vijay Shekhar Sharma steered Indian fintech's boldest turnaround storyPaytm reported its first full-year profit of Rs 552 crore in FY26, capping a sharp reset under Vijay Shekhar Sharma. The numbers shift attention from last year's regulatory shock to cost discipline, UPI growth and an AI-led strategy.

Your office commute is getting costlier. Is 'work from home' the answer?Getting to office is starting to feel expensive. With fuel price hike concerns growing and salaries already stretched by inflation and EMIs, employees are increasingly asking whether work from home deserves a second look.

China shows interest in US oil after Trump-Xi talks on Iran and Strait of HormuzAccording to a White House readout, Trump and Xi agreed that the Strait of Hormuz should be reopened to support global energy needs. The waterway has effectively remained closed since the start of the US and Israel conflict with Iran.

Sensex ends 790 points higher, Nifty above 23,600; IT stocks tumbleThe S&P BSE Sensex surged 789.74 points, or 1.06%, to close at 75,398.72, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 277 points, or 1.18%, to settle at 23,689.60.

8th Pay Commission: Why government employees want DA merged into basic payEmployee unions have requested the 8th Pay Commission to absorb Dearness Allowance (DA) into basic pay. The move could alter salary calculations, allowances, pensions and retirement benefits for lakhs of employees.

Bedridden, but asked to visit bank for re-KYC? Why this keeps happening in IndiaMany customers complain that despite digital infrastructure existing on paper, some branches still insist on physical presence, hard-copy paperwork, or repeated document submissions.

8th Pay Commission update: Employee body pushes for 50% guaranteed pension and moreAs discussions about the 8th Pay Commission continue, a key employee body has put forward a set of demands aimed at improving retirement security. At the centre of it is a proposal for an assured pension under the National Pension System (NPS).

India seeks extension of US waiver on Russian oil amid Iran conflict: ReportThe waiver, initially approved by the US in March and later expanded through an authorisation valid until May 16, allowed Indian refiners to continue receiving already-loaded Russian cargoes.

Why PM is asking Indians to cut spending: NITI Aayog explains austerity messageAs tensions in West Asia continue to rise, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to be careful with spending and avoid unnecessary expenses. But why now? Is this simply a precautionary step or a sign of deeper economic worries? NITI Aayog Vice Chairman Ashok Lahiri explains what the Prime Minister's austerity message really means.

Damage is now evident: Market strategist Akshay Chinchalkar on oil, gold, rupeeIn an interaction with IndiaToday.in, Akshay Chinchalkar, Managing Partner and Head of Markets Strategy at The Wealth Company, explains rising market stress, rupee weakness, soaring gold prices and investor fears.

If not physical gold, then what? Why experts are backing ETFs as an alternativeWith gold prices surging after the import duty hike, investors are increasingly turning towards ETFs as a cheaper, more flexible alternative to physical bullion amid rising uncertainty and rupee weakness.

Why Trump took Musk, Tim Cook, Jensen Huang, other top CEOs to ChinaTravelling with US President Trump are some of the world's biggest corporate names, including Elon Musk, Jensen Huang, Tim Cook and Larry Fink, along with executives from companies such as Meta, Visa, Goldman Sachs, Boeing and Mastercard.

Why did India ban sugar exports? Here's what's happeningThe ban applies to exports of raw, white and refined sugar, although shipments already in the export pipeline under specific conditions will still be allowed. Certain quota-based exports to the US and European Union remain exempt.
Sensex jumps over 300 points, Nifty above 23,500; Airtel up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 391.36 points to 75,000.34, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 148.65 points to 23,561.25 as of 9:36 am.

Sensex, Nifty today: Will stock market crash or open high today?Dalal Street is set for a higher opening after a mild rebound, with GIFT Nifty pointing above Wednesday's close. The recovery remains tentative as a record-low rupee, high crude prices, persistent foreign selling and elevated volatility keep sentiment fragile.

Gold gets costlier after PM Modi's appeal. Is petrol, diesel next?After the government raised gold import duty, fresh attention has turned to petrol and diesel prices as crude stays high. Remarks by PM Narendra Modi, the RBI and the IMF have sharpened concerns that consumers may not stay protected indefinitely.

entertainment.headline: My Royal Nemesis review: Lim Ji-yeon turns K-drama chaos into pure entertainmentRelease Date:Fri May 08, 2026 My Royal Nemesis is a wildly entertaining fantasy rom-com that throws every classic K-drama trope into one chaotic but addictive package. The Netflix drama turns that culture clash into brisk comedy, romance and questions about how history remembers women.

Troll says nobody noticed Alia at Cannes, actor gives epic reply Alia Bhatt replied to an Instagram troll after a Cannes clip claimed she was ignored by international paparazzi. Her quip drew praise from fans and kept the spotlight on her festival appearances.

Mera Lyari, Pakistan's reply to Dhurandhar, pulled from theatres after poor opening Pakistan's much-hyped film Mera Lyari, opened to a poor response and was reportedly pulled from several theatres on release day. The film was initially described as the country's answer to Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar film series.

Delhi CM Rekha Gupta praises Aakhri Sawal, calls it a powerful take on RSS journey Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta attended the Delhi premiere of Aakhri Sawal with the cast and crew. She later described it as a meaningful effort to present the RSS's 100-year journey.

Ayodhya has chosen me: Ramayana star Ranbir Kapoor buys land in city Ranbir Kapoor has purchased a piece of land in Ayodhya's The Sarayu project, adding to the growing celebrity interest in the temple town as he gears up for his next film, Ramayana.

Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar announce separation after 4 years of marriage Actor Mouni Roy released a statement on Thursday confirming her separation from husband Suraj Nambiar. The two have appealed to all to respect their privacy.

Rohit Saraf steps into Hanu-Man world with Mahakali, begins Hyderabad shoot Rohit Saraf makes his debut in Telugu films with Mahakali, the new film in the Prasanth Varma Cinematic Universe. The actor joins Akshaye Khanna and Bhoomi Shetty in the film's cast.

Off Campus review: A predictable but addictive hockey romance bingeRelease Date:Wed May 13, 2026 Prime Video's Off Campus turns Elle Kennedy's beloved BookTok romance into a glossy binge-watch packed with hockey boys, emotional chaos, slow-burn chemistry and steamy college drama. But does the adaptation live up to the hype?

Athiradi review: Basil and Tovino's film is imperfect, but full of heart and humourRelease Date:Thu May 14, 2026 Arun Anirudhan's Athiradi follows Samkutty as he joins BCET College to revive Arohan after a deadly stampede shut the fest down. The film draws its strength from campus nostalgia, humour and the bond between two brothers, despite uneven writing elsewhere.

Suriya fans protest in Puducherry as Karuppu release faces last-minute delay Suriya fans in Puducherry protested outside theatres after several Karuppu shows were cancelled on release day. The disruption turned planned first-day celebrations into confusion as the film's release was pushed to May 15.

Suriya's Karuppu not releasing today, team targetting May 15: Theatre owner Suriya's Karuppu did not release on May 14, with theatres cancelling all shows and issuing refunds. The delay, linked to reported financial issues, has pushed the team to seek distributor clearance for May 15.

Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Exam, Inspector Avinash S2, Nemesis and more OTT platforms are bringing a fresh lineup of thrillers, crime dramas and emotional stories this week, including Exam, Inspector Avinash: Season 2 and Nemesis. From socially relevant narratives to gripping cop dramas and glamorous fashion-world stories, viewers have plenty to add to their watchlist.

We meet again: Matt Reeves unveils The Batman: Part II cast in a unique reveal Matt Reeves shared the cast of The Batman: Part II through a series of social media posts in a one-of-a-kind reveal. He paired each post with a short message addressed to the returning actors' characters.

With you: Dulquer Salmaan, Vignesh Shivan support RJ Balaji amid Karuppu delay Actors and filmmakers Dulquer Salmaan, Vignesh Shivan and others posted messages backing RJ Balaji after Karuppu shows were cancelled on release day. The support came as reports linked the delay to unsettled dues.

Calm before the storm: NTRNeel new still released ahead of first glimpse. See here A new still of NTRNeel was unveiled on May 14 ahead of its first glimpse, set to be released on May 19. The film marks the first collaboration between Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel.

Indian Institute of Zombies review: A potentially fun comedy lost in translationRelease Date:Fri May 15, 2026 Indian Institute of Zombies follows a campus immortality experiment that unleashes a zombie outbreak. The film's promising satire and genre ambition are undercut by weak writing and tonal confusion.

Are you done now?: Visibly irked Alia Bhatt reacts as she gets mobbed at Cannes Alia Bhatt appeared visibly upset at the Bharat Pavilion in Cannes after fans and photographers crowded around her. The viral video has emerged as a major talking point from her latest festival outing.

Netflix this week: Kartavya, Desi Bling, Soul Mate and more Netflix is bringing a fresh lineup of releases this week, including Saif Ali Khan's action drama Kartavya and the glamorous reality-style series Desi Bling. From crime thrillers and emotional dramas to fantasy adventures, the platform has something for every kind of viewer.

Taylor Swift and Sabrina Carpenter win top songwriting honour at BMI Awards Singers Taylor Swift and Sabrina Carpenter shared one of the biggest honours at the 2026 BMI Pop Awards, alongside major wins for other top names in music.

general news.headline: Strong buzz at petrol stations; staff expect stiff Rs 15 hike in fuel pricesPanic buying and long queues were witnessed at petrol pumps across several Indian cities amid fears of a sharp fuel price hike triggered by rising crude oil prices and tensions in West Asia. An India Today ground report found consumers rushing for full-tank refills, while some pumps in states like Rajasthan and Gujarat even displayed "No Fuel" and "No Stock" boards.

Judge steps away from liquor policy case, but says not giving in to KejriwalDelhi High Court judge Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma recused herself from the excise policy case after initiating criminal contempt proceedings against Arvind Kejriwal and other AAP leaders over remarks targeting the court.

How Vijay's anti-liquor push mirrors his Master arcFrom reel-life reformer to real-life politician, Vijay's latest anti-liquor push feels strikingly familiar to those who've followed his films. The move mirrors the disruptive, system-challenging energy that defined his role in Master.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in India colours as BCCI names squad for tri-seriesThe Indian Cricket Board has named the India A squad for tri-series against Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. Tilak Varma has been named as the captain of the 15-member squad.

The NEET-UG whistleblower who reached Sikar police station in the dead of nightA whistleblower alerted the NTA about the question paper leak, which led to the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 exam. The exam for medical aspirants was held across India on May 3. At 1.30 am on May 4, the whistleblower went to a police station in Sikar with the guess papers as evidence, but left abruptly. This is what happened.

US moving to end $265 million fraud cases against Gautam Adani: ReportThe US Justice Department is reportedly set to drop criminal fraud and bribery charges against Gautam Adani linked to an alleged $265 million corruption scheme tied to India's largest solar power project, according to a Bloomberg report.

Troll says nobody noticed Alia at Cannes, actor gives epic replyAlia Bhatt replied to an Instagram troll after a Cannes clip claimed she was ignored by international paparazzi. Her quip drew praise from fans and kept the spotlight on her festival appearances.

ED arrests Kolkata DCP Santanu Sinha in alleged extortion caseThe ED suspects that Kolkata Police Deputy Commissioner Santanu Sinha Biswas was allegedly involved in running an extortion syndicate with the help of "Sona Pappu" and Jay Kamdar, the managing director of Sun Enterprise.

Why China brings out a toned down TrumpTrump's tone just changed in Beijing, where China projects itself as an equal power centre that can hit back. From trade and rare earths to Iran and global markets, Beijing today holds key leverage that even Trump cannot ignore.

MLAs favoured KC Venugopal, yet he was passed over for Kerala CM. Here's whyEven as most Congress MLAs reportedly rallied behind KC Venugopal, the party leadership ultimately chose the leader it believed best captured Kerala's public mood.

Truth has triumphed: Arvind Kejriwal after judge transfers case to another benchArvind Kejriwal on Thursday said "truth has triumphed" after Delhi High Court judge Justice Swarna Kanta Sharma transferred the excise policy case to another bench following contempt proceedings against him and other AAP leaders over remarks targeting the judge.

Mumbai family celebrates son's 55% exam results with marksheet-themed cakeMohammed Zaid's family was elated after his school exam results came out. Though he scored 55 percent, the family celebrated the occasion with a custom cake designed like a marksheet.

Mouni Roy, Suraj Nambiar announce separation after 4 years of marriageActor Mouni Roy released a statement on Thursday confirming her separation from husband Suraj Nambiar. The two have appealed to all to respect their privacy.

Live: No foreign trips for a year for Delhi ministers in step with PM's appealPM's forex-saving push Live Updates: Amid a global oil supply crunch and rising fuel prices, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged citizens and government departments to cut unnecessary expenditure and focus on savings. Stressing the need for economic resilience during a period of energy uncertainty, the Prime Minister called for prudent fuel consumption and the efficient use of resources to reduce the impact of the ongoing oil crisis on households and the broader economy. Following this, state governments and multiple departments have begun chalking out directives in this direction. Stay tuned to India Today as we bring you the latest developments.

Indian cargo ship sinks after drone strike near Oman, Delhi calls attack deplorableCargo ship Haji Ali was passing near Limah, off Oman's northern coast, at around 3:30 am on Wednesday when it was struck by an unidentified explosive object, suspected to be a drone or missile.

Banter or racism? Arshdeep calls Tilak Varma 'oye andhere', faces backlashPunjab Kings fast bowler Arshdeep Singh has landed in trouble over his social media videos once again. In a video posted on Snapchat, Arshdeep can be heard calling Tilak 'andhera', which loosely translates to dark.

Vikas Khanna, Ranveer Brar and now Harsh Goenka wowed by Odia flavoursOdia cuisine is one of the most underrated, but with time, it is slowly getting its due. The most recent acknowledgement came from industrialist Harsh Goenka.

2-day WFH, no-car day, online meets: Delhi's move after PM's save fuel appealIn step with the PM's save fuel appeal, the Delhi government announced that it will observe a "Metro Day", and also appeal to people to follow a "No Car Day" once every week.

What is Thucydides Trap that Xi warned Trump about?As US-China tensions deepen over trade, technology, and military power, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned his American counterpart, Donald Trump, to avoid the Thucydides Trap. What is the Thucydides Trap, and what does it have to do with rivalry and conflict?

What happened to Prateek Yadav? Doctor, autopsy reveal hidden health battlePrateek Yadav's doctor said he left the ICU against medical advice despite repeated warnings. His postmortem later attributed his death to massive pulmonary thromboembolism, ruling out steroid speculation.

The great capri comeback is here. Thanks to Alia, Khushi, and KendallAlia Bhatt arrived at the French Riviera for the 79th Cannes Film Festival in sleek black capris. Her look has renewed attention on capri trousers as fashion's latest nostalgia-led comeback.

We asked top banks how they fight digital fraud. None respondedIn the third and final part of the series, Banking Risks in Digital India, IndiaToday.in reached out to major banks to understand how they are preparing for the next generation of digital fraud — from deepfakes and mule accounts to coercion-driven scams and AI-enabled deception. None of the banks responded.

Fame Gurukul nostalgia hits hard as Arijit Singh-Qazi Touqeer reunite in JiaganjSinger Qazi Touqeer shared a video revisiting his journey with Arijit Singh, from their Fame Gurukul days to a recent visit to Jiaganj. The clip captures moments from scooty rides around the town to a friendly game of cricket, offering a nostalgic glimpse into their bond.

sports.headline: PR Sreejesh raises tough questions for Hockey India after controversial coaching exit PR Sreejesh has questioned Hockey India's coaching structure and growing reliance on foreign staff after his controversial exit from the junior men's setup despite a successful 18-month tenure.

Top government officers, bureaucrats enjoy sports fund meant for athletes An investigation by The Indian Express has alleged that crores from the National Sports Development Fund were spent on sports infrastructure linked to bureaucrats and exclusive government institutions instead of athletes and public sporting projects.

Did not expect Test captaincy before Mumbai Indians role: Jasprit Bumrah IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians handed Jasprit Bumrah the captaincy of the team for the first time in the Indian Premier League. At the toss, Bumrah joked that he did not expect to become the India Test captain before leading MI.

Jannik Sinner unstoppable, eclipses Novak Djokovic to set Masters world record Italian Open 2026: World No.1 Jannik Sinner broke Novak Djokovic's record for the most consecutive wins in Masters 1000 events after defeating Andrey Rublev in the quarter-finals at Rome

Big blow for Nikhat Zareen: Boxing star set to miss out on Asian Games Nikhat Zareen lost 1-4 to Sakshi Chaudhary in the 51kg semi-finals at the Patiala trials on Thursday. The defeat rules the two-time world champion out of the Asian Games qualification.

Banter or racism? Arshdeep calls Tilak Varma 'oye andhere', faces backlash Punjab Kings fast bowler Arshdeep Singh has landed in trouble over his social media videos once again. In a video posted on Snapchat, Arshdeep can be heard calling Tilak 'andhera', which loosely translates to dark.

The Premier League Weekly: Dreams, doubts and drama in the final stretch Arsenal's title hopes are alive again as Mikel Arteta's side regain momentum, but the Premier League drama stretches far beyond the summit. From Chelsea's managerial uncertainty to Manchester United's transfer dilemmas, the intrigue keeps building.

Drug ban setback to IPL resurgence: Kagiso Rabada recounts emotional 12 months After going through the lows of a drugs ban and the highs of winning an ICC title with South Africa, Kagiso Rabada said the difficult year taught him not to care too much about criticism and outside opinions.

PBKS vs MI LIVE: Dharamsala clash set for thrilling finish with MI needing 15 off 6 PBKS (Punjab) vs MI (Mumbai) Live Score, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians (173/4 in 18 overs) chasing 201 vs PBKS. Tilak Varma and Will Jacks have kept Mumbai Indians afloat in their 201 chase. Despite the required rate climbing, Tilak and Jacks haven't thrown in the towel just yet.

Italian Open match halted as Inter Milan's fireworks celebration sweeps Rome arena Italian Open 2026: The quarterfinal clash between Luciano Darderi and Rafael Jodar was temporarily halted after smoke from Inter Milan's Coppa Italia celebrations drifted into Rome's Foro Italico and disrupted visibility.

CSK sign South African all-rounder as replacement for injured Jamie Overton Chennai Super Kings have signed South African all-rounder Dian Forrester as a replacement for injured England all-rounder Jamie Overton for the remainder of the Indian Premier League 2026 season.

India won't play in FIFA World Cup 2026, but 3 Indian-origin players just mightIndia may not be on the plane to the 2026 World Cup, but the tricolour connection is far from broken. From a 19-year-old sensation from Kerala to a former Bayern Munich prodigy, three Indian-origin footballers could make history on the world stage. As global heavyweights finalise their squads, they discover the stars carrying the flag for the diaspora in North America.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi in India colours as BCCI names squad for tri-seriesThe Indian Cricket Board has named the India A squad for tri-series against Sri Lanka A and Afghanistan A. Tilak Varma has been named as the captain of the 15-member squad.

Nahid Rana crowned ICC Player of the Month for stunning New Zealand displaysBangladesh pacer Nahid Rana won the ICC Men's Player of the Month award after spearheading Bangladesh's ODI series victory over New Zealand.

Golden chance for Arshdeep Singh to find form: S Badrinath ahead of MI clashS. Badrinath challenged Arshdeep Singh to rediscover his best against Mumbai Indians, dismissing fatigue concerns and calling the PBKS pacer's form crucial in the race for the IPL playoffs.

Madonna, BTS and Shakira to headline first World Cup final halftime showFIFA has confirmed that Madonna, Shakira and BTS will headline the first-ever halftime show at the FIFA World Cup 2026 final at MetLife Stadium.

Thailand Open: PV Sindhu, Satwik-Chirag storm into quarter-finalsPV Sindhu and the men's doubles pair of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy and Chirag Shetty advanced to the quarter-finals with commanding second-round wins at the Thailand Open.

Axar Patel, Rahane, Pant face IPL captaincy axe after disastrous campaignsAxar Patel, Rishabh Pant and Ajinkya Rahane could lose their captaincy after poor IPL campaigns, sources tracking franchise developments told PTI. The scrutiny has sharpened as playoff hopes fade and teams assess leadership, selections and coaching support.

Virat Kohli's century proves old guard still runs the show, says Sunil GavaskarSunil Gavaskar hailed Virat Kohli's unbeaten century against KKR as proof that the old guard still has plenty to offer, with the RCB star silencing doubters, smashing records and punishing KKR for an early dropped chance.

Pat Cummins slams rumours of leaving BBL for SA20 over payment disparityPat Cummins has rubbished reports suggesting Australian players may prioritise SA20 over the BBL from 2028, as the debate over franchise pay gaps and Cricket Australia's contract structure intensifies.

Hardik will leave MI after IPL 2026: Ex-India captain makes bold predictionKris Srikkanth has claimed Hardik Pandya will leave Mumbai Indians after IPL 2026. MI have struggled under Hardik's captaincy and suffered an early exit from IPL 2026.

After Sreejesh's fiery exit, Hockey India appoint Soyez as Jr.team coachHockey India appointed Frederic Soyez as coach of the junior men's team a day after PR Sreejesh's exit from the role. Soyez comes in with a big reputation, having worked as the head coach of Spain and France before.

If you drop Virat Kohli in a chase, bound to get difficult: Abhishek NayarRCB vs KKR: Abhishek Nayar admitted KKR paid the price for dropping Virat Kohli, while also pointing to Varun Chakravarthy's absence as a crucial factor in the six-wicket defeat to RCB.

Told you so: Krunal Pandya's prediction for Virat Kohli comes true in RaipurRCB vs KKR: Krunal Pandya's faith in Virat Kohli paid off spectacularly as the RCB star bounced back from back-to-back ducks with a match-winning century against KKR in Raipur. Kohli's stunning knock not only powered RCB to the top of the table but also shattered multiple T20 records.

PBKS vs MI, Dharamshala weather: Will rain spoil Punjab's playoff chances?Punjab Kings and Mumbai Indians head to Dharamsala with both sides short on momentum. For Punjab, the bigger concern is not just rain but a campaign wobbling at the wrong time.

PBKS vs MI: Punjab Kings can't stop the rumours. They seek to stop the rotPBKS vs MI, IPL 2026 preview: Punjab Kings have answered the noise on social media, backed their players and drawn a line. Now, at a rain-soaked Dharamsala, the only statement that actually matters is the one they make on the field against Mumbai Indians on Thursday.

City smash Palace to keep pressure on Arsenal in Premier League title raceManchester City breezed past Crystal Palace at home to keep pressure on Arsenal in the Premier League title race on Wednesday, May 13. City cut the gap to Arsenal to two points.

Virat Kohli's double act: When the human and the superhuman showed up in RaipurIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: After back-to-back ducks, Virat Kohli walked out in Raipur nervous, celebrated a single like it was a six, and then scored a match-winning hundred. One night, two sides of the most compelling cricketer of his generation were on full display.

IPL Play of the Day: Kohli, Padikkal return as RCB's perfect chase comboRCB vs KKR: Whenever Virat Kohli and Devdutt Padikkal settle into an IPL 2026 chase together, panic slowly disappears from the RCB dugout. One controls the tempo, the other destroys the pressure, and Bengaluru suddenly make big run chases look far easier than they should.

Virat Kohli's special night lights up Raipur with plethora of records: In StatsIPL 2026, RCB vs KKR: Virat Kohli smashed a sensational century and shattered multiple records as Royal Challengers Bengaluru climbed to the top of the table with a six-wicket win over Kolkata Knight Riders on Wednesday in Raipur.

Virat Kohli no longer chasing records, just soaking in cricket before it endsIPL 2026: Virat Kohli struck an unbeaten 105 as RCB beat KKR by six wickets in Raipur. After reaching multiple milestones, he said he now wants to savour every day left in cricket.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
